chunk_summary: This section discusses fat-soluble vitamin dosing and monitoring in pediatric cholestasis, emphasizing nutritional management in chronic liver disease and referencing key clinical guidelines.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of fat-soluble vitamins in managing pediatric cholestasis.
Identify appropriate dosing and monitoring strategies for fat-soluble vitamins in chronic liver disease.
Recognize the impact of cholestasis on micronutrient absorption and liver function.
Apply evidence-based guidelines for nutritional support in pediatric liver disease.
key_facts: Cholestasis impairs absorption of fat-soluble vitamins (A, D, E, K), necessitating supplementation.
Vitamin A deficiency can lead to night blindness; monitoring serum retinol levels is recommended.
Vitamin D supplementation should aim to maintain serum 25-hydroxyvitamin D levels >30 ng/mL.
Vitamin E deficiency is associated with neurological symptoms; alpha-tocopherol levels should be monitored.
Vitamin K is critical for coagulation; supplementation may be required to correct prolonged prothrombin time.
Guidelines recommend individualized dosing based on severity of deficiency and liver function.
reference_quote: Cholestasis significantly impairs the absorption of fat-soluble vitamins, necessitating careful monitoring and individualized supplementation strategies. (Source: NASPGHAN and ESPGHAN Joint Position Paper)
document_key_topics: Nutritional management in pediatric liver disease
Guidelines for vitamin dosing and monitoring
